text:
The World War II (1939-1945) was an international war in which majority of the world nations participated and it emerged to be the deadliest war ever known in the history of humankind. It started in Europe but soon spread to Asia, Africa and the oceans of the world. In essence, the war was the result of unresolved questions of World War I, stringent stipulations of the Treaty of Versailles, the Great Depression and the emergence of the totalitarian regimes that were aggressive in nature in Germany, Italy and Japan.

The war broke out on the 1 st of September 1939, when Nazi Germany (led by Adolf Hitler) invaded Poland. Britain and France retaliated and declared war against Germany. During the two years, Germany employed a war concept known as Blitzkrieg (lightning war) to overpower most of Europe including France, the Low Countries, and some of Scandinavia. Italy was the ally of Germany and Axis powers appeared to be unstoppable in the continent.

In the meantime, in Asia, Japan was making years-long expansion, invading Manchuria in 1931 and China in 1937. In search of resources and control of the Pacific, Japan wanted to have an empire. December 7, 1941, It was a surprise airstrike by Japan on the United States at Pearl Harbor, Hawaii. This completely engaged the U.S. in the war as an ally of the Allies that was made up of Britain, the Soviet Union (after the invasion by Germany in 1941), China, and other numerous countries.

The World War II was fought in several fronts. Germany lost with the massive battles at Stalingrad and Kursk in Europe taking its toll. The Soviet Union had a massive burden on the combat and losses. In North Africa, there was an Allied Army that combated the Germans and the Italians before finally invading Italy. The Allies landed in Normandy on June 6, 1944, D-Day, opening up a Western Front and slowly moving to assault Germany on the west before the Soviets struck the same on the east.

The brutality against civilians was of extreme nature in the war. The holocaust, the organized massacre of six million Jews and millions of other people including Roma (gypsies) and disabled individuals, political dissidents and prisoners of war was committed by the Nazi regime. Camps of concentration and extermination as Auschwitz were synonymous with mass killing and hate of race.

In the Pacific the U.S and its allies fought against Japan in the small hopping campaigns and were able to take control of major places such as Guadalcanal, Ivo Jima and Okinawa. These wars were so bloody and demonstrated how Japan would fight in order not to be defeated. The United States made an invasion of the home islands of Japan, which would have been expensive to do, by dropping atomic bombs on Hiroshima and Nagasaki in August 1945 in order to end the war sooner. Soon afterwards Japan capitulated.

Germany capitulated already in May 1945 in Europe, and Hitler committed suicide and Soviet troops occupied Berlin. World War II was over as both Germany and Japan were defeated.

The consequences were vast. Millions of people were displaced, dozens of millions of people were killed, dozens of cities were destroyed. The establishment of the United Nations was done in an attempt to curb the global wars that would occur in future. The war also predetermined the Cold War between the Soviet Union and the United States as both of them became superpowers having the different political and economic systems.
